sentence: The meeting was ______ due to bad weather.
explanation: El phrasal verb <call off> significa cancelar. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <called off> (pasado de <call off>).
tip: The meeting was cancelled because of the weather.

sentence: She ______ a brilliant idea.
explanation: El phrasal verb <come up with> significa idear o proponer. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <came up with> (pasado de <come up with>).
tip: She had a brilliant idea — she invented it.

sentence: We need to ______ expenses this month.
explanation: El phrasal verb <cut down on> significa reducir.
tip: Spend less money this month.

sentence: I'm ______ the summer holidays.
explanation: El phrasal verb <look forward to> significa esperar con ilusión. En la oración se usa la forma continua: <looking forward to> (de <look forward to>).
tip: You feel excited about the holidays — you can't wait.

sentence: Can you ______ my cat while I'm away?
explanation: El phrasal verb <look after> significa cuidar.
tip: Take care of the cat while the owner is away.

sentence: She ______ her mother in many ways.
explanation: El phrasal verb <take after> significa parecerse a un familiar.
tip: She resembles her mother — they look alike.

sentence: The plane ______ an hour late.
explanation: El phrasal verb <take off> significa despegar. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <took off> (pasado de <take off>).
tip: The plane left the ground an hour late.

sentence: I need to ______ how to fix this.
explanation: El phrasal verb <work out> significa resolver o calcular.
tip: Find the solution — solve the problem.

sentence: He ______ the job offer because the salary was low.
explanation: El phrasal verb <turn down> significa rechazar. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <turned down> (pasado de <turn down>).
tip: He said no to the job offer.

sentence: Please ______ your hand if you know the answer.
explanation: El phrasal verb <put up> significa levantar.
tip: Raise your hand to answer the question.

sentence: The fire ______ after the rain started.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go out> significa apagarse (fuego/luz). En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <went out> (pasado de <go out>).
tip: The fire stopped burning after the rain.

sentence: She ______ with her boyfriend last month.
explanation: El phrasal verb <break up> significa terminar una relación. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <broke up> (pasado de <break up>).
tip: Their relationship ended last month.

sentence: I ______ an old friend at the supermarket.
explanation: El phrasal verb <run into> significa encontrarse con alguien por casualidad. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <ran into> (pasado de <run into>).
tip: I met an old friend by accident at the supermarket.

sentence: He ______ that the report had several errors.
explanation: El phrasal verb <point out> significa señalar o indicar. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <pointed out> (pasado de <point out>).
tip: He mentioned that the report had errors.

sentence: We ______ the meeting until next week.
explanation: El phrasal verb <put off> significa posponer.
tip: The meeting was delayed — moved to next week.

sentence: She ______ getting the job after all.
explanation: El phrasal verb <end up> significa terminar haciendo algo. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <ended up> (pasado de <end up>).
tip: In the end, she got the job — that was the final result.

sentence: I need to ______ my sleep this weekend.
explanation: El phrasal verb <catch up on> significa ponerse al día.
tip: You need to recover lost sleep this weekend.

sentence: The teacher ______ the exam papers.
explanation: El phrasal verb <hand out> significa repartir. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <handed out> (pasado de <hand out>).
tip: The teacher distributed the exam papers to students.

sentence: He ______ university in his second year.
explanation: El phrasal verb <drop out of> significa abandonar los estudios. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <dropped out of> (pasado de <drop out of>).
tip: He left university — he quit his studies.

sentence: Let's ______ the plan one more time.
explanation: El phrasal verb <go over> significa repasar.
tip: Review the plan one more time — check it again.

sentence: She ______ an interesting topic during the meeting.
explanation: El phrasal verb <bring up> significa mencionar un tema. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <brought up> (pasado de <bring up>).
tip: She mentioned a topic — she raised the subject.

sentence: We need to ______ a solution to this problem.
explanation: El phrasal verb <figure out> significa resolver o entender.
tip: Find the answer — understand how to solve it.

sentence: The concert was ______ because of the storm.
explanation: El phrasal verb <call off> significa cancelar. En la oración se usa el pasado simple: <called off> (pasado de <call off>).
tip: The event was cancelled due to the storm.

sentence: He always ______ doing his homework until the last minute.
explanation: El phrasal verb <put off> significa posponer.
tip: He delays his homework — he does it at the last minute.

sentence: I need to ______ my sister from the airport.
explanation: El phrasal verb <pick up> significa recoger a alguien.
tip: Go to the airport to collect your sister.
